edited.text: Yeah, some of them are about your size—it's a wide mix. Baboons are probably as tall as you, or a little shorter. The gorillas are clearly bigger. Orangutans are a little bigger. Chimps and bonobos are smaller, shorter, stockier. And right over there are definitely about seven foot—he's just kind of… yeah, right, to a couple of them. The most important thing I want you all to notice is that as they're fighting, he is saying, "Save your strength—don't use it to harm yourselves; I have plenty more where they came from," as he's throwing copper out there. You notice the Charau-ka are not hurting each other for this—they're like, "Oh, you beat me to it, I'll grab another one." They are not killing each other over this copper. But you also notice that the Charau-ka love this little bit of competition, this little bit of frenzy. This goes on for a whole thirty seconds.
